element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns swing mechanics with standard club before encountering varied club requirements
Strategic planning tool available before each shot
Power meter timing introduced immediately, fundamental mechanic
Environmental variable affecting ball trajectory after basic swing mastery
Distinct perspective shift for short-range precision gameplay

element_introduction_order.element: Multiple course layouts (3 courses in international version)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Available from start (US Course, Japan Course, UK Course)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Different hole designs and difficulty variations across courses

element_introduction_order.first_appears: International version only (not in Japanese release)

typical_relief_triggers: Transition from difficult hole to easier course section
typical_tension_triggers: Difficult course layouts (Japan Course more difficult than US Course)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based rhythm with player-controlled shot pacing; tension builds during each hole's challenges, releases upon completion; longer courses create sustained challenge across multiple holes

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Course selection menu provides three difficulty levels via different courses; Japan Course represents harder optional challenge compared to US Course

difficulty_curve.early_game: US Course serves as introductory difficulty level with simplified hole layouts; players learn basic swing mechanics, power meter timing, and bird's-eye view course reading
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Japan Course introduces increased complexity; same holes rearranged and some modified to increase difficulty; wind mechanics more pronounced; caddie tips become essential strategy tools
difficulty_curve.late_game: UK Course provides capstone challenge; tournament mode (international version) extends progression through multiple rounds of competition
notable_spikes: Transition from US Course to Japan Course shows significant difficulty increase
Japanese original version (Mario Open Golf) was harder overall than international release, with different hole difficulty balancing
Tournament mode in international version creates sustained challenge across multiple rounds

notable_level_patterns.description: Three courses available from start in international version (US, Japan, UK) with escalating difficulty. Japanese version required course unlocking with Japan Course as only starting option. Holes reordered between versions to balance difficulty for target audience.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: US Course (90 holes in Japanese version distributed as 54 holes internationally)

notable_level_patterns.description: Every hole combines bird's-eye view for planning with third-person swing perspective for execution and top-down view for putting. This creates three distinct spatial grammar systems within each hole.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Japan Course holes (shares layouts with Australian and French courses in original)

notable_level_patterns.description: Wind and terrain affect each shot differently across courses. International version modified hole difficulty by adjusting environmental factors (wind speed, terrain slope) rather than redesigning layouts.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Holes shared between Japanese Australia Course and International US Course (hole 1 of Australia = hole 9 of US Course)

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - NES Open Tournament Golf
